prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is in black and white and is shown through a series of close-up shots focused on writing materials and hands.

At the beginning, a hand is handling or opening a stack of papers or envelopes against a dark background. The person appears to be wearing a dark sleeve with striped cuffs, possibly a uniform-style garment. The hand is sorting through or lifting a piece of paper.

The next shot shows a close-up of an envelope being held in a hand. The envelope has a postage stamp in the upper right corner and a handwritten address. Some of the writing is legible, including “Mr. Jack Hudson” and “San Francisco, Calif,” though other parts are unclear.

Later, the commercial cuts to an extreme close-up of a fountain pen nib. The pen tip is shown very prominently, angled toward a light writing surface. Details of the metal nib and feed are visible.

In the final shot, a hand writes on a sheet of paper with a fountain pen. The paper contains lines of cursive handwriting, though the text is not fully readable. Large overlaid text across the lower half of the frame reads “SHEAFFER’S,” identifying the product or brand.
